questions.q: Panchavati in the Ramayana is best described as what?
options: A forest home where Rama lived in exile
questions.explanation: Panchavati was the forest grove where Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana built their hut and lived during their exile.

questions.q: On the banks of which river was Panchavati located?
questions.explanation: Panchavati was a grove on the banks of the Godavari river.

questions.q: Which three figures lived together at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Rama, his wife Sita, and his brother Lakshmana made their forest home at Panchavati.

questions.q: What did Rama and Lakshmana build for the family to live in at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana built a simple hut of wood, leaves, and thatch for them at Panchavati.

questions.q: Panchavati was a place that Rama visited during which period of his life?
questions.explanation: The family settled at Panchavati during the years of Rama's exile in the forest.

questions.q: The name 'Panchavati' refers to a grove of which kind of feature?
questions.explanation: The name Panchavati literally means a place of five (pancha) banyan or fig trees (vati).

questions.q: What was the general mood of life at Panchavati before trouble arrived?
questions.explanation: The family lived in peace and happiness at Panchavati until trouble came.

questions.q: Which demoness's arrival shattered the peace at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: The arrival of the demoness Shurpanakha set off the chain of events that ended their peace at Panchavati.

questions.q: Panchavati is located in which broad region of the forest the exiles wandered?
questions.explanation: Panchavati lay within the vast Dandaka forest where Rama spent much of his exile.

questions.q: Who is the central hero whose forest home Panchavati became?
questions.explanation: Panchavati was the forest home of Prince Rama during his exile.

questions.q: On whose advice did Rama choose to settle at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya advised Rama to make his dwelling at Panchavati on the Godavari.

questions.q: Which noble vulture befriended Rama in the region of Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Jatayu, the aged king of vultures, befriended Rama near Panchavati and later tried to save Sita.

questions.q: Shurpanakha was the sister of which demon king?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha, who came to Panchavati, was the sister of Ravana, the demon king of Lanka.

questions.q: What did Shurpanakha desire when she approached Rama at Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha was smitten with Rama and wished to marry him when she came to Panchavati.

questions.q: How was Shurpanakha disfigured during the episode at Panchavati?
options: Lakshmana cut off her nose and ears
questions.explanation: At Rama's direction, Lakshmana cut off Shurpanakha's nose and ears when she attacked Sita.

questions.q: After being humiliated at Panchavati, to which brothers did Shurpanakha first run for revenge?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha fled to the demons Khara and Dushana, who attacked Rama with their army.

questions.q: Single-handedly at Panchavati, Rama destroyed an army of how many demons sent by Khara?
questions.explanation: Rama alone slew the fourteen thousand demons of Khara's army in the battle at Panchavati.

questions.q: It was from Panchavati that Sita was eventually carried off by whom?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducted Sita from the hut at Panchavati after luring Rama and Lakshmana away.

questions.q: Which demon took the form of a golden deer to lure Rama away from Panchavati?
questions.explanation: The demon Mareecha disguised himself as a golden deer to draw Rama away from the hut at Panchavati.

questions.q: Seeing the golden deer at Panchavati, what did Sita ask Rama to do?
questions.explanation: Sita was enchanted by the golden deer and begged Rama to catch it for her at Panchavati.

questions.q: In what guise did Ravana approach Sita's hut at Panchavati to abduct her?
questions.explanation: Ravana came to Panchavati disguised as a wandering mendicant or ascetic to deceive Sita.

questions.q: Which protective boundary did Lakshmana draw before leaving Sita alone at Panchavati, per popular tradition?
questions.explanation: In popular tradition Lakshmana drew the protective Lakshmana Rekha line around the hut at Panchavati.

questions.q: Panchavati is described in the Aranya Kanda, which Kanda of Valmiki's Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Panchavati episodes are narrated in the Aranya Kanda, the third book of Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: When the false deer was struck, in whose voice did it cry out to deceive Sita and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: The dying Mareecha cried out in Rama's voice, tricking Sita into sending Lakshmana away from Panchavati.

questions.q: Cut down while trying to stop Ravana's flight from Panchavati, Jatayu had what relationship to King Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was an old friend of Dasharatha and fought Ravana to defend Sita near Panchavati.

questions.q: Besides Khara and Dushana, which third demon general commanding Khara's host fell to Rama near Panchavati?
questions.explanation: Trishira, along with Khara and Dushana, led the demon force that Rama destroyed at Panchavati.

questions.q: Shurpanakha first turned her wrath on Sita at Panchavati because she viewed Sita as what?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha attacked Sita out of jealousy, seeing her as the rival standing between her and Rama.

questions.q: When first rebuffed by Rama at Panchavati, to which brother did Shurpanakha then turn her advances?
questions.explanation: After Rama deflected her, Shurpanakha turned to Lakshmana, who mockingly refused her at Panchavati.

questions.q: Hearing of his army's destruction at Panchavati, Khara's defeat ultimately prompted which demon to seek revenge?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha carried news of Khara's destruction at Panchavati to Ravana, inciting him to abduct Sita.

questions.q: The modern town associated with Panchavati lies near which present-day Indian city on the Godavari?
questions.explanation: Panchavati is traditionally identified with a locality near Nashik in Maharashtra on the Godavari river.
